title: Error while reading an Excel file written with OpenXML SDK
author: OSjoerdWie
body:
OpenXML SDK generates valid output, yet without a global namespace. For example, the contents of xl/workbook.xml could look like the following

`<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="true"?>
<x:workbook xmlns:x="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main" xmlns:r="http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/officeDocument/2006/relationships">
<x:fileVersion rupBuild="9302" lowestEdited="4" lastEdited="5" appName="xl"/>
<x:workbookPr defaultThemeVersion="124226" filterPrivacy="1"/>
<x:bookViews>
  <x:workbookView activeTab="1" firstSheet="1" windowHeight="8010" windowWidth="14805" yWindow="105" xWindow="240"/>
</x:bookViews>
<x:sheets>
  <x:sheet r:id="rId2" sheetId="1" name="Sheet1"/>
  <x:sheet r:id="rId3" sheetId="2" name="Sheet2"/>
  <x:sheet r:id="rId4" sheetId="3" name="Sheet3"/>
</x:sheets>
<x:definedNames>
  <x:definedName>....</x:definedName>
</x:definedNames><x:calcPr calcId="122211"/>
</x:workbook>`

Upon loading an xlsx file with this workbook content, the parsing of xl/workbook.xml results in an undefined workbook. This is probably due to the fact that node matching is done without taking into account namespaces.

comments.author: visnup
comments.body: I've run into the same issue. For example, this file from Microsoft's test suite fails: https://github.com/OfficeDev/Open-XML-SDK/blob/main/test/DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Tests.Assets/assets/TestFiles/Spreadsheet.xlsx.
